zh.lifeSummary: 當Hawise de Burgh於1256年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德時，她的父親John de Burgh20歲，母親Cecily de Balliol19歲。她於1278年之前與Robert Grelley III結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1299年3月24日之前在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hawise de Burgh于1256年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾登東納爾德时，她的父亲John de Burgh20岁，母亲Cecily de Balliol19岁。她于1278年之前与Robert Grelley III结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1299年3月24日之前在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
meanings: This female name (which has been corrupted down to Avice and Avis) is from Hadewisa, formed from the German name Hedvig, same as Edwig, which Wachter translates 'propugnator felicitatis;' say, 'fortunate soldier.'

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Hawise de Burgh was born in 1256, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, John de Burgh, was 20 and her mother, Cecily de Balliol, was 19. She married Robert Grelley III before 1278. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died before 24 March 1299, in Manchester, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hawise de Burgh est née en 1256, à Dundonald, Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Burgh, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Balliol, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Grelley III avant 1278. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 24 mars 1299, à Manchester, Lancashire, Angleterre.

Sie hat vor 1278 Robert Grelley III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 24. März 1299 in Manchester, Lancashire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Hawise de Burgh(이)가 1256년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Burgh(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Balliol(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1278년 이전 Robert Grelley III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1299년 3월 24일 이전 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hawise de Burgh родилась в 1256 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Burgh было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Balliol, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Grelley III перед 1278 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 24 марта 1299 г. в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия.
